prompt: Set up a BrewFlow Offline Retail & Training Suite that enables a beverage shop to take in-store pickup orders while running an internal barista knowledge program, all from a single English UI. Customers browse products organized by SPU/SKU and choose required options (size, milk type, sweetness level) with real-time price updates and sales tax displayed before checkout; store hours and pickup windows are shown in 15-minute slots, and items that require prep time block out slots that are too soon. At checkout, the user receives an on-screen reservation voucher code and a countdown hold timer; holds default to 10 minutes and clearly indicate when an item will be released if not confirmed. Staff use a separate interface to move each order through a production/fulfillment state machine (Accepted, In Prep, Ready, Picked Up, Canceled), scan or manually enter the voucher at arrival, and see mismatch warnings if the wrong order is presented. Admin, Academic Affairs, and Teacher roles manage a question bank and mock exams used for training: authorized users import question sets from local CSV files, generate timed mock exams by subject, difficulty, and chapter, and review score analytics, favorites, and a wrong-answer notebook with smart review sessions that prioritize repeated mistakes. The application supports Chinese/English language switching, localized dates and times, and multilingual, SEO-friendly URLs and sitemaps suitable for an intranet kiosk deployment. The backend uses Rocket to expose offline REST-style APIs consumed by the Dioxus frontend, with MySQL for durable local persistence of users, roles, products, option sets, carts, reservations, vouchers, orders, fulfillment events, questions, exam versions, attempts, and analytics snapshots. Authentication is strictly local username/password with salted hashing and a 12-character minimum password policy; sessions use signed, rotating cookies with a 30-minute idle timeout, and sensitive fields such as voucher codes are stored encrypted at rest and masked in logs. Space reservation enforces concurrency locks per SKU option combination to prevent overselling, releases inventory automatically at hold expiry, and blocks cancellation after Ready unless the user has Admin privileges. Task matching and dispatch is implemented for staff assignment within the store using locally stored station zones and shift windows (no maps): the engine recommends work based on zone, time window, current workload, and a reputation score, and supports both grab-order and assigned modes with queue controls to prevent double acceptance. For stability, the system includes anti-crawling and fault-tolerance mechanisms for any internal scraping/import routines (proxy pool support for lab environments, dynamic user agents, cookie/session management, redirect parsing, reserved CAPTCHA integration points kept disabled by default), plus health checks, exponential backoff, and automatic degradation that temporarily disables non-critical background jobs while preserving ordering and reservation integrity.
